bead.title: review: bounded evidence assembly with minimum floor + pre-dispatch short-circuit
bead.description:
Stage C1 of FEAT-022. Modify the single review-prompt assembler
(post-collapse agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to use cli/internal/evidence
primitives for bounded assembly with a minimum evidence floor and
pre-dispatch short-circuit on residual oversize.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §5 and §7 for the reviewer path.
Satisfies US-220 and contributes to US-225.

Depends on Stage B (ddx-c924af97) for the single assembler and Stage A
(ddx-64299657) for primitives.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (BuildReviewPrompt and
  gitShow: route governing-doc reads through evidence.ReadFileClamped,
  route diff through evidence.ClampDiff + evidence.DecomposeDiff,
  extend EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs as needed, implement priority-
  ordered assembly)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (wire pre-dispatch short-
  circuit: if assembled prompt exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all
  trimming, skip provider dispatch and emit review-error:
  context_overflow directly; do NOT close the bead)
- Tests with multi-MB synthetic governing docs and multi-MB synthetic
  diffs exercising: minimum floor preservation (bead title, description,
  AC, notes, full changed-file inventory always present); governing-doc
  degradation to outline + first-N-lines when content exceeds cap; diff
  ranking (files named in AC > files matching governing ref paths >
  others); large-file degradation to stat + hunk headers; pre-dispatch
  short-circuit path.

Out-of-scope:
- Grading path (Stage C2, separate bead)
- Section-level telemetry (Stage F)
- Error-class distinction for non-overflow classes (Stage G); this bead
  introduces context_overflow as the only new class it needs
- Markdown-aware heading extraction (deferred per FEAT-022 §1; use
  line-based type-agnostic FitSections)

Preserve invariant: reviewer failure does NOT close the bead. The
pre-dispatch short-circuit emits the event and leaves the bead in the
same state as any other review-error (closed status gated elsewhere).
bead.acceptance: cd cli && go test -run TestBuildReviewPrompt ./internal/agent/... passes including new tests for: governing-doc trimming (fixture doc = 10× MaxGoverningDocBytes, assert output bounded + truncation marker present), diff trimming (fixture diff = 10× MaxDiffBytes), minimum floor preservation under extreme pressure (fixture total input = 20× MaxPromptBytes; output still contains bead.Title, .Description, .Acceptance, .Notes, and full changed-file inventory verbatim), ranked diff inclusion (AC-referenced files appear before unreferenced files in output order), large-file stat+hunk-header degradation (single fixture file > MaxInlinedFileBytes → output contains stat line and hunk headers but not full content), pre-dispatch short-circuit. cd cli && go test -run TestReviewContextOverflow ./internal/agent/... passes: given a fixture that forces residual overflow, the executor emits a review-error event whose body contains the literal substring 'context_overflow' and the event's recorded provider-dispatch count remains 0 (no provider call happened). cd cli && go test -run TestReviewFailureNotCloseBead ./internal/agent/... still passes (the existing execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go must remain green — preservation of the non-close invariant). AST/import check (not a grep): cd cli && go test -run TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage ./internal/agent/... asserts that execute_bead_review.go imports cli/internal/evidence and that the file's AST contains no direct calls to os.ReadFile or osexec.Command("git", "show", ...) unless a parent node carries an //evidence:allow-unbounded directive comment.

governing.title: Feature: Prompt Evidence Assembly and Size Invariants
